rationale_for_baseline_tier: Severe decompensating hypothyroidism with an early myxoedema-coma edge — bradycardia (~48 bpm), hypothermia (~35.8 C) and altered mentation (apathy, slowed hoarse speech) — plus ten days of obstipation with a distended, sore abdomen carrying impaction/obstruction risk, in a housebound patient presenting out-of-hours. The source note mandated immediate out-of-hours assessment to assess cardiovascular stability, exclude bowel obstruction and prevent progression to myxoedema crisis. Telehealth cannot measure vitals, examine the abdomen, take bloods or exclude early myxoedema coma, so the safe disposition is urgent in-person emergency assessment (T4). It is not T5/000 by default because the patient is conscious and communicating with a carer present, but a 000 escalation edge is armed for any reduction in consciousness, worsening hypothermia, collapse or signs of complete obstruction.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Becoming drowsy, unrousable or confused, worsening shivering/feeling colder, or collapse
escalation_edges.rationale: Reduced consciousness with hypothermia and bradycardia indicates myxoedema coma — a life-threatening emergency needing ambulance transport and hospital resuscitation (warming, IV levothyroxine/hydrocortisone).
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she becomes drowsy, hard to wake, more confused, colder, or collapses, call 000 immediately and tell them it may be a thyroid emergency. Keep her warm and stay with her.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe or sharp abdominal pain, relentless vomiting, or a rigid distended abdomen
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — call 000 or go to the nearest emergency department
escalation_edges.rationale: These signal complete bowel obstruction or perforation, a surgical emergency, on a background of severe faecal loading.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the tummy pain becomes severe or sharp, she starts vomiting and can't stop, or her belly goes hard and rigid, call 000 or get to an emergency department straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe hypothyroid symptoms without yet-present emergency features — the baseline presentation
escalation_edges.rationale: The current picture already warrants urgent in-person emergency assessment because early myxoedema and obstruction risk cannot be excluded remotely.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Because of how slowed-down, cold and blocked-up you are, you need to be seen in person urgently tonight — please arrange emergency assessment now rather than waiting for a clinic.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Housebound and profoundly fatigued, but a roommate is present and able to help and monitor
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Enlist the roommate to arrange transport to urgent assessment, keep the patient warm, and watch for the 000 triggers; the patient cannot safely self-transport unaided.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Roommate is the effective informant and monitor
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give the roommate the explicit escalation triggers and ensure they can call 000 if the patient deteriorates.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: You need urgent in-person assessment tonight — please arrange emergency care now rather than waiting for a routine appointment. Your very slow heart rate, low body temperature, how slowed-down you've become and ten days without a bowel motion all need to be checked in person with blood tests. Keep warm, keep your roommate with you, and do not take any more senna.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Attend the nearest emergency department now, or call 000 if you cannot get there or if you deteriorate
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: This is an out-of-hours presentation and must not wait for clinics to open — emergency assessment is available 24 hours a day.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Provide the emergency team and GP with the timeline: one month of cold intolerance, weight gain despite poor appetite, profound fatigue and hoarse slowed speech; ten days of obstipation; and the request for urgent TSH/free T4/anti-TPO. Thyroxine to be commenced by the clinician once bloods confirm the diagnosis.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating this as routine hypothyroidism/constipation and routing to a routine GP or pathology appointment in days.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Progression to myxoedema coma (stupor, respiratory and cardiovascular collapse) or complete bowel obstruction/perforation before assessment.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Arranging urgent same-day assessment but without emergency-department capability or without arming the 000 escalation triggers, risking delayed recognition of deterioration to myxoedema crisis.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling an emergency ambulance immediately for a conscious, communicating patient with a carer present when urgent in-person assessment would suffice; clinically defensible given the myxoedema edge, so weighted far less than under-triage.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot measure the heart rate, temperature or thyroid bloods, or examine the abdomen, so it cannot confirm hypothyroidism or grade the decompensation remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Act on the pattern and the red flags: arrange urgent in-person assessment with confirmatory bloods, safety-net for myxoedema and obstruction, and withhold remote thyroxine.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Either falsely reassuring the patient and deferring to a routine appointment, or 'confirming' hypothyroidism and starting/dosing thyroxine without bloods.
